review_text: Amazing. Leaves me in a good emotional space.
There are lesbians. 10/10.

Trippy and wild with more plot twists than a twizzler, multiverses have never been so fun or engaging. But this movie is more than just a thrill ride, it has a brain and soul and won best picture for 2023. Props to Michelle Yeoh who won best actress, Jamie Lee Curtis who won best supporting actress and Ke Huy Quan who won best supporting actor. Yeah it's that good.

Loved the comedy in this movie. Action isn’t usually my thing, but here it worked really well. The sci-fi elements were great, and the surreal moment with the hot-dog fingers was especially memorable. Writing this review a long time after seeing it, and that scene still sticks with me.

Everything Everywhere All at Once (2022) It's simply crazy and fun at the same time. Until now, I've never seen a movie about the Multiverse as bizarre as this one. It's simply captivating and brilliant.
Everything is overwhelming. Kindness is everywhere, and you can do it all at once.

I just finished watching Tetris last night that came out in 2023, the same year this mess "Everything Everywhere All At Once" came out. It was only by chance I seen a Youtube Tetris short clip and decided to watch it. Tetris based on a true story was invented during The Cold War in Russia and wasn't even considered for an Oscar and yet it is the best film I have seen in years. Just look at the youtube comments on the Tetris trailer. It is quite clear that an Oscar is worth nothing and just goes to the highest bidder. Everything Everywhere All at once was just that... all over the place. Over rated garbage. I lasted an hour watching it... I advise everyone to watch Tetris to restore your faith in what it means to be brave and courageous to stand up against Communism. Maybe if Hollywood had got their hands on Tetris it would not have become the masterpiece that it is. Tetris should be mandatory viewing for every child in America and Europe as a warning to the horrors of Communism.

I 100% agree with the comment that said "WOW WOW WOW WOW WOW JUST BRILLIANT" and I say it again: WOW WOW WOW WOW WOW. JUST B-R-I-L-L-I-A-N-T. One of the best and coolest pieces of art I’ve ever watched and seen

DEEPLY-MOVING, TRANSCENDENTAL, AND ALLURING
Akin to a 2 hour root canal sans lidocaine!

Imagine "The Matrix" on acid—this film takes you on a mind-bending ride from start to finish. Humour, imagination, and creativity grab you by the sausages and hurtle you through a fascinating and emotional journey. The first film I've seen to visualise compassionate killing, this hit me deep. I came out with a wet face, a hopeful heart, and a throbbing snakepit of energetic and vibrant ideas!

Don't watch it you're wasting your time! Terrible acting, shitty storyline, made no sense and wasn't even funny. I honestly don't understand how people thing it's great because it's so overrated and doesn't deserve awards.
This movie is complex, heartbreaking, funny, action packed with a deeply emotional yet uplifting undertone. A must watch of the 21st century.

Everything Everywhere All at Once is truly everything everywhere all at once. The movie attempts at a lecture of life. Whilst the attempt in itself is not bad, it fundamentally fails to portray any meaningful message regarding it. Outside of the lacklustre attempt to teach a lesson of value, the film forgets to include the most fundamental component of film-making: stake. Albeit the excellent action sequences and flamboyant camera work, the reason this film inherently fails to be interesting is due to a lack of any stake within the movie. There is no "risk" being associated with any of the characters within the film, as they can simply just co-exist within another "multiverse". Hence, the audience presumably has difficulty attaching to any set character within the film, given no character is truly at risk of "failing". The irony is, that for a film that is so geared towards teaching a life lesson, it forgets to include the most important lesson of life: taking risks.
It’s official, people are stupid…worst movie I have seen that has an Oscar, by far……

Breaks the tired plot stereotypes of modern movies in a wonderful way. When you're sure the movie is going to zig, it doesn't zag -- it turns around and runs completely the other way! It's refreshing to see something so out of the box. And take a veneer of a dark action film and turn it into the most heartwarming film I've seen in years.

If I could give this Zero Stars I would. I had big expectations and it didn't meet any of those. Found absolutely nothing funny about it, and when it first started I was like okay. This could be good and then it took a downhill turn and it just kept rolling through the ditch.

It’s similar to the concept of the Matrix from Kung Fu Hustle, with the elevation of one’s own values, but it has a lot of cringe-worthy moments and a messy plot. I don’t understand where the rating and the Oscar came from.
Creativity, Imagination, and story telling at it's best.
This film was truly chaotic nonsense. It was with to sit through to the end.
Truly a masterpiece and work of art.
This is so chaotic it gave me migraine.
Nonsensical, boring, obnoxious.

Wow. This movie is wild. It’s crazy, funny, emotional, and completely unpredictable all at once. Michelle Yeoh is amazing—she makes all the weird action, the jumping between universes, and the really emotional family stuff feel completely real. The movie jumps around a lot, and sometimes it’s kind of overwhelming, but that’s also what makes it fun. It’s like a rollercoaster where you don’t know what’s coming next, but you want to stay on for the whole ride. I laughed, I cried, and honestly, I just kept thinking about it long after it ended. If you like movies that are weird, kind of messy, but really smart in a sneaky way, you’ll probably love this one.

Abysmal in every sense of the word. A mess particularly the Asian laundromat trope. Disappointing to see Michelle Yeoh in a role like this. Right there with Joy Luck Club for bad Asian stereotyping to top off a muddled mess of a film. Cant believe I wasted 2 hours(Ok it was on an airplane) watching this drivel.

Talented cast who give charming performances... or at least as much as is possible within the confines of this vehicle. It's just not a motion picture. An art piece? I tried to suspend disbelief but couldn't become engaged in the gibberish.

Had to create an account just to rate it. One of my favorite movies of all time. You’ll feel every emotion during thins watch. I was crying out of my eyes, pores, you name it, I was crying out of it. The message was as very profound and resonates with me a lot. Watch it!

Amazing movie. The acting, direction, and cinematography were all brilliant. The first time I watched it, there were parts I missed and didn’t fully understand, but after a second viewing it became an incredible experience. I recommend that viewers take their time with this film and rewind any scenes they find confusing. Also, try not to multitask while watching, or you may end up just as confused as I was during my first viewing.

great film with lotsa twists and turns. but why give an oscar to jamie lee curtis who was only in the film for like only 15 mins? stephanie hsu, who played the daughter, had much more screen time & also played multiple deep characters, should've received the oscar. they must have felt sorry for jamie lee who's mostly an aged scream queen, plus hollywood royalty, being the daughter of tony curtis& janet leigh.

I knew nothing about this movie before watching, and boy, was I taken for a ride. This is very surreal, weird and funny, with lots of action sequences and alternate universes. But through all the craziness, the story is really about a family that needs to reconnect. The three actors in the family give fantastic performances, as does Jamie Lee Curtis as a Karen-esque IRS agent. I have never seen a movie like this before, and probably never will. If you decide to watch, just keep an open mind and let it take you for a spin. It is crazy, humorous, touching, sad, exciting, fast-paced and somewhat relatable despite all that.

There are only a few films in my favorite top 20 list which are less than 20 years old. This film is one of those few. The thing that makes this movie stick out is its relevance to the modern moment. Its conflict is rooted in the conflict of the meta experience that we living today experience constantly. Not only does this movie identify and provide an icon metaphor for the dilemma of nihilism infecting so many, but the story provides an answer that feels true, achievable and emotionally resonate.
It’s like a bad episode of Rick and Morty but with more Asians and lesbians.

It's like someone tried to mix Monty python, Dr Strange and Bruce Lee - and did not quite carry it off. The eventual message of kindness is OK but getting there takes a lot of perseverance. How this got seven Oscars (I like Jamie Lee too but seriously?) I will never know.

I understand why some people love it but just too chaotic for me. The mix of metaverse, fantasy, martial arts and talking rocks was just too much. Michaelle Yeoh is terrific. I can't fathom how Jamie LC got an oscar for her part. It was total nonsense. Ultimately a satisfying story which hung together well in spite of all the jumping to different 'verses' but too much going on; like being on a non stop rollercoaster for 2 and a half hours.

An incredible film that captures the different perspectives of a daughter, and does so in a very entertaining way. It also shows how important the mother's relationship is, and how much she did to be with her daughter. The father is incredibly tender. Overall, amazing.
Outstanding. Incredibly fast-paced and clever. The imagination in the concepts rendered are off the charts.

Not my cup of tea. Some of the acting was good, but the whole premise was too far fetched. The movie jumped too much from scene to scene and was literally strobe like in places. The totally unrealistic choreographed martial arts "fight" scenes were ridiculously unbelievable.

Really hard to watch, couldn't get through it. It's like everyone in it is drinking Red Bull on the hour every hour. It's a chaotic mess, to the point where it just gets repetitive and boring. Not sure how this got such good reviews.

Imagining the endless possibilities that could come from making a key few different choices at a key few different times opens up a massive can of existential worms that Everything Everywhere All at Once dives headfirst into.

absolutely loved it. shudders all around. transcended the limits of my imagination to convey one strong, universal theme, that personally resonated with me (even more so at the time i was watching it): the relationship between mother and daughter.

Three hours of absolute torture. For me this was a bunch of nonsensical footage thrown together as part of a barely comprehensible “story” with a bunch of misfit characters that were supposed to be comical. The “special effects” have been done before. Cloud Atlas was way better. For me this was not even a film.
Blew my mind. I rarely rewatch movies but I'll probably have to this time.
Really good story, and incredibly good visuals. Comedy and action in this film are really executed.

Amazing movie. In the begining I felt lost and confused but then the story takes over and the Directing shines in a complex and original story telling. It all flows together and becomes like its title. The acting was superb as well as the dialogues that become more then moving the story forward. I loved it and can only recommend for every open minded movie lover.
